description_long: This sculpture features a central column adorned with four prominent horse figures, each with a human figure riding or interacting with it. The horses are arranged symmetrically around the column, creating a dynamic sense of movement. At the top of the column sits a smaller, detailed figure, possibly a cherub or mythological character, holding an object. The base is square and appears to be made of stone or concrete, providing stability and contrast to the more intricate upper section. The surface of the sculpture has a weathered, patinated appearance, suggesting it is made of bronze or a similar metal. The overall design is ornate and classical, likely intended as a decorative focal point in a public or park setting.
description: An ornate, classical sculpture with four horse figures and human riders arranged around a central column, topped by a smaller figure, all set on a square stone base.
description_view_0: This view highlights the side of the sculpture with two horses and their riders visible, as well as the top figure in profile.
description_view_1: This view shows the front, with two horses facing outward and the top figure directly visible, emphasizing the symmetry.
description_view_2: This view presents the opposite side from view 0, showing the other two horses and riders, with the top figure turned away.
description_view_3: This view is similar to view 1 but with brighter lighting, making the details of the horses and riders more pronounced.
attribute_location_context: No specific context is visible, but the sculpture appears suited for an outdoor urban or park environment.
